specReference: C Style — 8-bit DOS aesthetic, sepia/gold monochrome, static non-scrolling layouts

screens.name: Title / New Game
recommendations: Title art is full-color illustration, not 8-bit sepia — strongest C Style deviation on entry
Invisible hit-target buttons work but offer no visual affordance; consider subtle outline in debug/audit mode
Continue disabled state is correct when no save exists

screens.name: Market Screen
recommendations: Excellent fixed three-zone layout — no scroll, full height utilization
Serif district headings and rounded borders diverge from pure DOS monospace spec
Goods icons are pixel-art gold — good C Style alignment
Market row BUY/SELL buttons are small (~24px height) — below 44px touch target guideline
Empty alt on goods icons hurts screen reader experience

screens.name: Market (with holdings)
recommendations: Hold column and green highlight communicate inventory clearly
SELL enabled/disabled states are intuitive

screens.name: Travel Screen
recommendations: Six equal-height district rows fit without scroll — meets spec
District thumbnails consistent size with market header
Current location grayscale/disabled treatment is clear
Travel car header image adds character; aspect differs from square district thumbs

screens.name: Trade Modal (Buy)
recommendations: Quantity presets (+1/+10/½/MAX) reduce friction
Inline error display (#terr) is good UX — bank modal should match
Modal scrolls on very small viewports (max-height 92dvh) — minor spec deviation

screens.name: Trade Modal (Sell)
recommendations: Avg cost and profit % display supports strategic decisions
Color-coded profit up/down aids readability

screens.name: Bank / Don
recommendations: Four-action grid is clear; debt interest warning prominent
Bank only at Little Italy is correct but not explained on this screen
Uses alert() for errors — inconsistent with trade modal
Borrow max shows per-transaction cap, not total debt picture

screens.name: Feds Encounter
recommendations: Split ev-popup layout with 8-bit art is strongest C Style execution in the app
Three combat options clearly labeled; disabled fight when unarmed
Consider aria-labels on action buttons for combat state

screens.name: Event Popup (Mugging)
recommendations: Event card art matches rare/super-rare visual language
Single amber CTA keeps flow fast

screens.name: Event Popup (Dead Drop)
recommendations: Consistent split-panel event pattern across all travel events

screens.name: Event Popup (Rare Intel)
recommendations: Per-event artwork elevates rare tier above generic fallback
Headline/body hierarchy readable at mobile sizes

screens.name: Market Anomaly Toast
recommendations: Shortage/frenzy/flood art distinguishes anomaly types well

screens.name: Tutorial Overlay
recommendations: Four-step onboarding covers debt, bank, guns, muggings — good coverage
Card-style tutorial differs from ev-popup event aesthetic
No skip button — Escape does not dismiss tutorial
Tip counter (1/4) helps orientation

screens.name: High Scores
recommendations: Scrollable table breaks static-layout spec
Visual treatment differs from end-game split-screen leaderboard
Consider reusing eos-row styling for consistency

screens.name: End Game
recommendations: Rank card + score split layout is distinctive and on-brand
Leaderboard section scrolls when entries exceed viewport — minor spec violation
Play Again / Main Menu actions clearly placed

globalRecommendations: Unify typography: choose either full monospace DOS or document serif as accent-only
Reduce border-radius on panels (8px → 2–4px) for sharper DOS box aesthetic
Increase market row button touch targets to minimum 44px height
Replace alert() in bank flow with inline errors
Add meaningful alt text to all commodity and district images
Align High Scores screen with End Game leaderboard component
